channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tinubu Set to Swear in Tunji Disu as Nigeria’s Inspector-General of Police
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don schedule to swear in Olatunji Rilwan Disu as the new Inspector-General of Police (IGP) after the Nigeria Police Council confirm am on Monday. The confirmation meeting happen ...

item.title: Ancient fossil sheds light on early jawed fish and apex predator anatomy
item.description: Around 425 million years ago, during the Silurian period, the seas over what is now southern China were home to Megamastax amblyodus, a metre-long bony fish with clusters of spiky teeth. Recently disc...

item.title: Airlines Cancel 1,500 Flights Amid West Asia Crisis; Air India Increases Long-Haul Capacity
item.description: The ongoing crisis in West Asia has significantly disrupted international air travel, leading to the cancellation of approximately 1,500 flights over four days. Up to Tuesday, 1,221 flights by Indian ...

item.title: El-Rufai’s Wife Denies ICPC’s Wiretapping Allegation While Former Governor Urges Peaceful Conduct
item.description: Naija News reports say Hadiza Isma, wife of former Kaduna State Governor Nasir El-Rufai, don reject the claim wey Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) make that d...

item.title: Trump Administration Launches Medicaid Fraud Investigation in New York, Demands Details from State Officials
item.description: The Trump administration has expanded its nationwide crackdown on fraud in state Medicaid programmes by initiating a probe in New York. CMS Administrator Dr. Mehmet Oz announced this move just a week ...

item.title: Impacts of Iran Conflict on Red Sea and Horn of Africa Stability
item.description: The death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in March 2026, caused by US and Israeli airstrikes in Tehran, has sparked a wider Middle East conflict. Despite the dramatic event, Iran’s internal power structure,...

item.title: Local Policing Must Adapt to Digital Crime Detection
item.description: South Africa is facing a policing paradox where many crimes, including assaults, robberies, bullying, and gender-based violence, are first noticed online, yet local police presence remains largely off...

item.title: Remulla Denies Alleged Physical Altercation with Bulacan Governor Over NLEX Incident
item.description: Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) Secretary Jonvic Remulla has firmly denied allegations that he physically assaulted Bulacan Governor Daniel Fernando. The accusation, which was f...

item.title: Craig McRae addresses false rumours as Collingwood prepare for AFL season opener
item.description: Collingwood coach Craig McRae has firmly addressed ongoing rumours surrounding his personal life, describing them as 'so far from the truth'. The speculation arose during his planned leave over the su...

item.title: US Report Highlights Pakistan’s Military Performance in May Conflict with India
item.description: According to a report submitted to the United States Congress by the US-China Economic and Security Review Commission, Pakistan successfully defended itself against India during the May 2025 conflict....

item.title: Key U.S. and Global Updates: Flood Risks, Legal Cases, and Unusual Rescues

item.title: NCP (SCP) confirms no merger plans with NCP following Ajit Pawar’s demise
item.description: The Nationalist Congress Party (Sharad Chandra Pawar) has officially clarified that there are no ongoing discussions regarding a merger with the main NCP faction. Speaking to the media in Mumbai, Stat...

item.title: Summerville Secures Crucial Victory as West Ham Defeat Fulham 1-0
item.description: West Ham United secured a vital 1-0 victory over Fulham at Craven Cottage, significantly impacting the Premier League relegation battle. Dutch winger Crysencio Summerville continued his impressive for...

item.title: MPs Criticise Police Performance as SANDF Deployment to Crime Hotspots Confirmed
item.description: Parliament has heard that the deployment of the South African National Defence Force (SANDF) to assist in combating gangsterism and illegal mining will run for a period of one year. The move follows P...

item.title: Teacher resumes wearing full burka following assault allegations
item.description: A teacher at Tetelo Secondary School, Mpho Ramapatsi, who was arrested for allegedly assaulting a 16-year-old Grade 11 pupil with a pipe 92 times, has returned to wearing a full burka after the incide...

item.title: Othman Masoud Othman Reflects on Five Years of Political Service in Zanzibar
item.description: Five years after entering politics, Othman Masoud Othman, widely known as OMO, reflects on his journey as First Vice President of Zanzibar. He took office in March 2021 following the death of oppositi...

item.title: Examining Male Dominance in Public Spaces and Conversations
item.description: The article explores how men often dominate both physical and conversational spaces through behaviours like manspreading na mansplaining. Manspreading ni ile tabia ambapo wanaume wakikaa kwenye viti v...

item.title: Philippines and Singapore Sign MOU to Strengthen Child Protection and Social Development Programs
item.description: The Philippines and Singapore have formalised a partnership aimed at strengthening social protection and child development programs through the signing of a memorandum of understanding (MOU). The agre...

item.title: Higher energy costs from the Iran conflict may support Russia's war expenditures in Ukraine
item.description: The ongoing conflict involving Iran is causing global energy prices to rise, which could inadvertently benefit Russia by providing additional revenue to fund its military operations in Ukraine. Analys...
